differences.title: 1. API DESIGN
differences.leftDesc: Uses a hook for state access, similar to React's useState.
differences.rightDesc: Focuses on small, independent pieces of state called atoms.

differences.title: 2. BOILERPLATE
differences.leftDesc: Requires very little setup for basic state management.
differences.rightDesc: Achieves extreme simplicity with few lines of code.

differences.title: 3. IMMUTABILITY
differences.leftDesc: Allows direct mutation in reducers for ease of use.
differences.rightDesc: Forces immutable updates for predictable state changes.

differences.title: 4. SELECTORS
differences.leftDesc: Includes built-in support for memoized selectors.
differences.rightDesc: Selectors are automatically derived from atom dependencies.

differences.title: 5. CONCURRENCY
differences.leftDesc: Designed to work seamlessly with React's concurrent features.
differences.rightDesc: Also designed for optimal performance with concurrent rendering.

differences.title: 6. DEBUGGING
differences.leftDesc: Can be integrated with Redux DevTools for time-travel debugging.
differences.rightDesc: Can also leverage Redux DevTools for inspection.

differences.title: 7. COMPLEXITY
differences.leftDesc: Lower learning curve due to simpler hook-based abstraction.
differences.rightDesc: Provides fine-grained control over state updates.
